item.title: Loved the last bit
item.content:encoded: It was the end that really made it for me, I was a bit meh up until that point, but then you had me. Well done! The one thing I might say is, would this really be classified as safe? It can't be contained And that last part means it would be a constant threat of exposing the foundation if it picked more dead agents. I feel like it might be euclid, but I'm new so I could totally be wrong. Either way, very cool!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Love this one! love anything radio related, and the "radio station from a different time and place but similar to our own" always draws me in. keep it up!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I should have announced this earlier, but I fixed a couple grammar issues a couple days ago.

item.title: The End
item.content:encoded:
As I was reading through it I was initially thinking, okay this is just another SCiP that can do some anomalous thing, nothing too special (not that they all have to be special). And I was gonna remain neutral until I opened the last bit. Very interesting, even gave me a little chill. Not necessarily scary in anyway, but definitely interesting.

+1

item.title: The title "Dead Air" was already taken
item.content:encoded:
"And four hours north of Portland
A radio flips on
And some no-one from the future
Remembers that you're gone"
-The Mountain Goats, Harlem Roulette

This is my first attempt at actually publishing an SCP in years of lurking and reading, and that's got me more than a little nervous. Still, I suppose everyone has to start somewhere. With thanks to LiterallyMechanical whose advice helped me tighten up the wonky description, and to my friends on discord who encouraged me to actually post something for once. A lot of the inspiration for this story came from 37 Short Films, one of my all-time favourite articles, as well as (obviously) Harlem Roulette. Here's hoping you enjoyed it!
